data.title: Shanghai Composite Index Opens 0.5% Lower at 3915.48
Shenzhen Composite Index Opens 0.5% Lower at 2537.05
ChiNext Price Index Opens 1.0% Lower at 3230.46
Thai prices hit 18-year low on surplus supplies; Indian rates slump
data.description:
Key points:
  • Thai prices hit lowest since November 2007
  • Indian prices quoted at $340-$345 per ton
  • Vietnam September exports drop 41.3% from same month last year

By John Biju

Thai rice export prices slumped to their lowest level in nearly 18 years this week, while Indian prices were at their weakest in more than nine years due to weak demand and strong supplies, with volatile local currencies also adding pressure.

Thailand's 5% broken rice (RI-THBKN5-P1) was quoted at $340 per metric tonne, slipping from last week's $345, a Bangkok-based trader said. Prices were at their lowest since November 2007.

"Supply is strong this year, thanks to good rainfall across all producing countries, but prices will not increase because buyers will only purchase what they need," said a trader, adding the strengthening baht had an impact.

The strong baht is impacting exports but if there is a chance of weakening, it would help, the trader said.

India's 5% broken parboiled variety (RI-INBKN5-P1) was quoted this week at $340-$345 per ton, the lowest levels since mid-2016. Prices have fallen from last week's $358-$365 range.

Indian 5% broken white rice was priced at $360 to $370 per ton this week.

Prices are falling as exporting countries compete for market share, giving buyers greater bargaining power, said a Kolkata-based exporter.

The rupee , trading near record low levels, also added pressure.

Vietnam's 5% broken rice (RI-VNBKN5-P1) was offered at $440-$465 per ton on Thursday, flat from last week, according to the Vietnam Food Association.

"Trade remains tepid due to thin demand as one of Vietnam's biggest buyers, the Philippines, is still suspending imports," said a trader based in Ho Chi Minh City.

Vietnam exported 483,000 metric tons of rice in September, down 41.3% from the same month of last year, government data showed.

For the January-September period, rice exports fell 1.6% from a year earlier to 6.9 million tons, with a value of $3.5 billion, it said.

Bangladesh has approved the purchase of 50,000 metric tons of Indian rice at $359.77 per ton through an international tender, part of a wider effort to stabilize domestic food prices amid ongoing volatility in global grain markets.

data.title: Top Glove's Shares Offer More Balanced Risk-Reward Profile — Market Talk
data.description: Top Glove's shares now offer a more balanced risk-reward profile following a recent correction and an improving sales outlook, Hong Leong IB analyst Chee Kok Siang says in a note. The glove maker targets monthly sales of about 12.9 billion units in 1Q FY 2026, up 10% on quarter, with average selling prices expected to be stable, he says. Raw material costs for natural and nitrile rubber should stabilize, while natural gas tariffs may decline about 4%, he says. Coming 1Q earnings could be stronger, supported by better operating leverage and lower costs, though uncertainty in the supply-demand balance heading into 2026 lingers, he adds. Hong Leong upgrades Top Glove to hold from sell and raises its target price to MYR0.67 from MYR0.60. Shares are 3.0% higher at MYR0.69. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan Offering Y4.3T Of TD-Bills (No 1337)
data.description:
TOKYO--Japan's Ministry of Finance said Friday it is auctioning Y4.3 trillion of treasury discount bills.

 
Issue                  TD-Bill (Number 1337) 
Settlement Date                 Oct 14, 2025 
Maturity Date                   Jan 19, 2026 
Offer Amount                   Y4.3 Trillion

This content was automatically published based on data and/or text from the original source. For feedback, write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com.

data.title: Japan MOF To Auction Y4.3T Of TD-Bills Oct 17
data.description:
TOKYO--Japan's Ministry of Finance said Friday it plans to auction Y4.3 trillion of treasury discount bills on Oct. 17, 2025.

 
Issue                        TD-Bill 
Auction Date            Oct 17, 2025 
Settlement Date         Oct 20, 2025 
Maturity Date           Jan 26, 2026 
Offer Amount           Y4.3 Trillion
